title: Battle of Mandalore (Yuuzhan Vong War)
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Battle of Mandalore}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Battle |prev= |conc= |next=*[[Defense of Gyndine]]''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' *[[Battle of Tholatin]] *[[Liberation of Ord Mantell]] *[[Battle of the Commenor system]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |image= |name=Battle of Mandalore |conflict=[[Yuuzhan Vong War]]{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} |date=c. [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]] The Mandalorians actively entered the war after the attack on Mandalore, and the [[battle of Caluula]] in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]] is the first battle with a confirmed date in which they participated. |place=[[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] |result=[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] victory''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[Yuuzhan Vong]] assault repelled *Mandalore's population reduced by one third; significant damage incurred to Mandalore''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' |side1=Mandalorians |side2=[[Yuuzhan Vong empire|Yuuzhan Vong Empire]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=[[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] |commanders2= |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*[[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]] *Numerous Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] and [[Mandalorian Supercommando|supercommandos]] *Mandalorian [[Starship/Legends|starships]] |forces2=*Yuuzhan Vong [[Warrior caste|warriors]] *Yuuzhan Vong [[Warship/Legends|warships]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=1.5 million Mandalorians, approximately one third of Mandalore's populace |casual2=High''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|You think anyone's stupid enough to try invading like the Empire did? After we kicked Vong ''shebs'' like that?|[[Goran Beviin]]|}} In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]], a '''battle''' was fought on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], between an attacking force of [[Warrior caste|Yuuzhan Vong warriors]] and the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] inhabitants of the world. During the early portion of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Yuuzhan Vong's invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the Mandalorians' [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|leader]], [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], had officially aligned the Mandalorians with the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] under the provision [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalorian space]] be spared from their conquests; however, Fett understood that the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] would eventually turn on the Mandalorians and secretly instructed his people to act as covert [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteurs]] and intelligence gatherers, turning over the information they collected to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. The [[Yuuzhan Vong empire|Yuuzhan Vong]] eventually uncovered Fett's deception, and launched an assault on Mandalore, [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombarding]] the planet with [[Singularity mine|singularity ordnance]] and landing [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]] by the thousand. The Mandalorians made use of underground tunnel networks and their fierce combat skills to engage the Yuuzhan Vong. At the [[City/Legends|town]] of [[Bralsin]], the Mandalorians clashed with more than a thousand Yuuzhan Vong warriors, [[Death/Legends|slaying]] them in droves. Though large swaths of [[Tree/Legends|forested]] land was leveled, and close to one and half million Mandalorians were killed by the battle's end, the Mandalorians succeeded in driving the Yuuzhan Vong from their world, becoming the only people of the galaxy to defeat the invaders on their own. The aftermath of the battle left Mandalore severely scarred, and the planet's infrastructure destitute until the discovery of a lode of [[Beskar/Legends|''beskar'']] [[Iron/Legends|iron]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] that had been exposed by the Yuuzhan Vong's bombardment. The highly valuable metal led to a period of economic resurgence for the Mandalorians, allowing Mandalore to make a steady recovery from the devastation the battle had left in its wake. ==Prelude== {{Quote|What do you have in mind? It'll take time to mobilize a whole army on Mandalore.''"
"''And we'll take massive casualties if we make a move before we know exactly what we're dealing with here. This is technology we've never seen before.''"
"''Sit and wait? You must be—''"
"''They fooled us. Now we fool them. We play nice and look like we're on their side while we gather intel until we have enough to hit them hard. We pretend to be in it for the money.|Goran Beviin and [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]|''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]''}} When the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] set out from their [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|home galaxy]] to conquer a [[The galaxy/Legends|new one]], they sent ahead a scout by the name of [[Nom Anor]] to make preparations that would allow their invasion to proceed more easily. Understanding the potential the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] had as allies, Anor made contact under the alias of "Udelen"; he first approached the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Goran Beviin]], alone, contracting the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] for an [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] against a [[Politics/Legends|politician]] on [[Ter Abbes]]. Satisfied with the display of Mandalorian competence Beviin showed upon completing the assignment, Anor met with the reigning [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']]—the traditional leader of the Mandalorian clans—[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to contract ongoing Mandalorian mercenary assistance in his secretive goals. Anor's true nature was revealed in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], when he gathered Fett and a small [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of Mandalorian-[[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to witness the arrival of the [[Yuuzhan Vong fleet]]. Confronted with the truth of the Yuuzhan Vong's [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] invasion, Fett negotiated the Mandalorians' continued support in exchange for immunity for the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]]. However, while the [[Yuuzhan Vong empire|Yuuzhan Vong]] and the galaxy at large believed the Mandalorians to be allied with the invaders, Fett ordered the Mandalorian people to covertly gather intelligence on the [[Alien/Legends|aliens]]' bio-techonology and [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] their efforts. Fett, himself, forged a secret relationship with the [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] [[Kubariet]], after rescuing the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] from a Yuuzhan Vong [[subaltern]] during their [[Battle of Birgis|attack]] on [[Birgis]]. [[Bur'lorr|The subaltern]] was [[Death/Legends|killed]], and Fett promised to pass on what intelligence his people collected to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] through Kubariet, so long as the Mandalorians were not publicly named as the source of the information. Despite these precautions, the Yuuzhan Vong eventually uncovered Fett's deception, and set out to wreak their vengeance on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]. ==The battle== {{Quote|I think there were a few thousand of them. And this was a poor place to defend, but we took them on.|Goran Beviin, discussing the battle with the Yuuzhan Vong at [[Bralsin]]|}} [[File:Boba-unifyingforce.jpg|thumb|right|140px|Boba Fett around the time of the battle]] In [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]], Yuuzhan Vong forces from the already conquered world of [[Bandomeer/Legends|Bandomeer]] launched an assault on Mandalore. Unlike other planets the Yuuzhan Vong had invaded with the intention to [[Terraforming/Legends|terraform]] them to their own specifications, the Yuuzhang Vong attacked Mandalore with the single purpose of destroying the Mandalorians. From [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], the Yuuzhan Vong's [[Warship/Legends|warships]] dropped [[Singularity mine|singularity ordnance]] indiscriminately, blowing massive craters in the surface of Mandalore. Large swaths of [[Tree/Legends|woodlands]] were flattened, including the forests that surrounded Mandalore's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] city, [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]]. Whole [[City/Legends|settlements]] of domains built into the trunks and branches of ancient [[veshok]] trees were obliterated in their entirety, and the buildings that comprised Keldabe sustained significant damage during the attack. Once fertile [[Farm/Legends|farmland]] was [[Poison/Legends|poisoned]] and [[Salt/Legends|salted]], in an attempt to render it unusable. The Mandalorians largely survived the [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital assault]] through the use of a network of underground tunnels that extended from beneath a number of their homes and structures, and into the mountains far away. When the Yuuzhan Vong deployed their [[Warrior caste|warriors]] to the surface, the Mandalorians struck back, using the tunnels and their sheer martial ability to [[Ground warfare/Legends|combat]] the Yuuzhan Vong. More than a thousand Yuuzhan Vong [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]] engaged the Mandalorians in the small Keldabe suburb of [[Bralsin]]. Despite the area's poor defensive capabilities, the Mandalorians fought and defeated them in a massacre that would leave an imprint in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] for more than a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] after. The Mandalorian soldier Goran Beviin, the first to make contact with the Yuuzhan Vong, killed several of their warriors at Bralsin with his ''[[Beskad/Legends|beskad]]'' [[Sword/Legends|saber]]. [[Legion/Legends|Legions]] of [[Mandalorian Supercommando|Mandalorian supercommandos]], native soldiers, and the [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]] led by [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], fought to protect Mandalore from the Yuuzhan Vong, eventually defeating and repelling the invaders. When the battle finally came to an end, close to one and a half million Mandalorians were dead, and though the Yuuzhan Vong death toll was higher still, Mandalore's population had been reduced by nearly a third. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|We lost nearly a million and a half people fighting the ''vongese''. That might be small change for Coruscant, but it's a disaster for us.''"
[...]
"''We lost a lot of our best troops, our farmland's been poisoned, and our industrial infrastructure is still shot to ''haran'' after ten years. So maybe this is the ideal time to bring some people home. Gather in the exiles while the rest of the galaxy is busy.|[[Neth Bralor]] and [[Graad]] note the damage the battle wreaked during a meeting of the Mandalorian clans|}} Regardless of the severe loss of life, the Mandalorians became known throughout the galaxy as the only people to defeat the Yuuzhan Vong on their own. Having turned openly hostile against the Mandalorians, Boba Fett led the Mandalorian Protectors against the Yuuzhan Vong in defense of the galaxy, [[Battle of Gyndine|defeating]] the Yuuzhan Vong at [[Gyndine/Legends|Gyndine]], [[Battle of Tholatin|liberating]] [[Tholatin]] from their control, and during the [[Battle of Caluula|battle]] at [[Caluula Station]], the Mandalorian Protectors made the Yuuzhan Vong fear the power of Mandalore.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' In spite of the Mandalorians' role in fighting the Yuuzhan Vong, when the war was over, Mandalore did not receive any reconstruction aid from the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], the newly risen galactic government formed during the conflict from a restructuring of the New Republic. More than ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the war's end, Mandalore's population remained largely depleted, many of its farms laid fallow, and tracts of land were still barren and [[Color/Legends|blackened]]. At the battlefield of Bralsin, the bodies of the dead Yuuzhan Vong were buried, though their [[Bone/Legends|bones]] often washed up following a heavy [[Water/Legends|rain]]. Keldabe's damaged buildings were mostly repaired over [[Time/Legends|time]], though the hundred-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-tall [[MandalMotors tower|tower headquarters]] of the [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]] company retained battle scarring a decade after the Yuuzhan Vong's assault. This [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] gave birth to the ''[[kadikla]]'' movement, espousing the need to place Mandalore first and concentrate on its reconstruction. In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], during a meeting of the clans at [[MandalMotors Hall]] in Keldabe, proponents of ''kadikla'' persuaded ''Mand'alor'' Boba Fett to recall two million Mandalorians from across the galaxy to rebuild the damage and revitalize the poisoned farms. At the ''Mand'alor''{{'s}} call, Mandalorians came by the hundreds, and upon approach to the planet, a pair of Mandalorians—one a [[Geologist/Legends|geologist]], and one a [[minerals engineer]]—discovered that the Yuuzhan Vong's singularity ordnance had exposed an enormous lode of [[Beskar/Legends|''beskar'']] [[Iron/Legends|iron]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] north of the city of [[Enceri]]. The ''beskar'' lode led to an era of economic resurgence for Mandalore, the Yuuzhan Vong's attack ironically strengthening the Mandalorian people. ==Behind the scenes== The Yuuzhan Vong's attack against Mandalore was first introduced to ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] via a mention in the [[Star Wars Insider]] article [[The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors|''The History of the Mandalorians'']], [[Author/Legends|authored]] by [[Abel G. Peña]] and published [[March]] [[2005]] as part of the Insider magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 80|eightieth issue]]. The battle and it's aftermath were later mentioned in [[Karen Traviss]]' ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' novels [[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice|''Sacrifice'']] and [[Legacy of the Force: Revelation|''Revelation'']], and alluded to it in her [[EBook|e-novella]], ''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]''. [[Jason Fry]] and [[Daniel Wallace]]'s reference book, ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', also makes note of the battle as part of the planetary entry for Mandalore, as does ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' in its informational entry on Mandalore. ==Appearances== *''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]'' {{C|Indirect allusion only}} *''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} {{1stm}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{YuuzhanVongWarNav}} {{Interlang |es=Batalla de Mandalore (Guerra Yuuzhan Vong) |pl=Bitwa o Mandalorę (29 ABY) }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mandalore (Yuuzhan Vong War), battle of}} [[Category:Battles of Mandalore]] [[Category:Battles of the Yuuzhan Vong War]] [[Category:Genocides and massacres]] [[Category:Orbital bombardments]]
